readme.purpose: Income from sources OUTSIDE the simulator accounts. Portfolio accounts handle their own distributions internally — do NOT re-enter those here.

format.ages: Recommended. Use age ranges: '47-64' means ages 47 through 64 inclusive. Converted to simulation years using current_age from person.json.
format.years: Legacy format. Year 1 = current_age + 1. Still supported for backward compatibility.
format.dollar_type: Optional. 'current' (default, unspecified) = today's purchasing power — the simulator inflates internally so the real value stays constant. 'future' = actual dollar amount received at that future date — simulator deflates to current USD. Use 'future' for fixed-dollar contracts (salary letter, lease, pension statement). Use 'current' for lifestyle-based planning ('I want to live on $150K/yr in today's money').

dollar_type_examples._comment: Current dollars (default) — real value held constant, nominal check grows with inflation
Future dollars — fixed contract amount, held flat in nominal terms

readme.what_not_to_enter: Do NOT enter: dividends or gains from accounts in allocation_yearly.json, RMDs (computed automatically), Roth conversion amounts. Entering these causes double-counting.
readme.gross_amounts: All amounts are GROSS (pre-tax). The simulator computes federal, state, NIIT, and Additional Medicare Tax.
readme.withdrawal_offset: Net income from these sources automatically reduces the portfolio withdrawal needed each year. If W2 + rental + SS net after-tax already covers your $150K spending target, the simulator draws $0 from the portfolio that year. Surplus (net income above target) is routed per excess_income_policy in economic.json.

income_types.w2: Gross wages or salary. Ordinary income tax rates. Also triggers Additional Medicare Tax (0.9%) on amounts above $250K MFJ / $200K single.
income_types.rental: Net rental income after expenses. Ordinary income rates. Does NOT trigger Additional Medicare Tax.
income_types.interest: Taxable interest from bank accounts, CDs, bonds outside portfolio accounts.
income_types.ordinary_other: Social Security (taxable portion), pensions, annuities, non-qualified distributions. If a social_security block is in person.json, that overrides ordinary_other for SS-active years (only for years where ordinary_other is zero — manual entries are preserved).
income_types.qualified_div: Qualified dividends from outside accounts (non-portfolio). Lower LTCG rates apply.
income_types.cap_gains: Realized capital gains from outside assets (property sale, non-portfolio investments). LTCG rates.
income_types.misc: One-off income not fitting other categories. Use for: gifts received, inheritances, bonuses, asset sale proceeds, insurance payouts. Each entry can optionally set 'taxable': false for non-taxable items (gifts, inheritances) — these still count toward surplus routing but are excluded from the tax engine.

misc_examples._example: Annual gift from parents ages 60-65 (non-taxable — under annual gift exclusion)
